title: Controller Design and Relaxation of Design Conditions for Takagi–Sugeno Fuzzy Systems
content:
This paper presents novel approaches to design robust controllers for Takagi–Sugeno (T–S) fuzzy systems, focusing on relaxing the restrictive design conditions typically encountered in traditional methods. The proposed methodologies aim to enhance the applicability and reduce the conservatism of T–S fuzzy control, particularly in scenarios where full state measurements are unavailable. 

Key contributions include the development of observer-based output feedback control strategies that guarantee stability and performance under various uncertainties. Mathematical derivations are provided, supported by Lyapunov-based stability proofs and Linear Matrix Inequality (LMI) formulations. The research demonstrates how these relaxed conditions lead to more feasible and less conservative controller designs, expanding the practical utility of T–S fuzzy systems in real-world engineering applications such as aerospace systems and mechanical control.
